title: India Fresh Passport Application (Ordinary, Adult, Citizen by Birth)
description: Apply for a first (never previously issued) Indian ordinary passport as an adult who is a citizen of India by birth — Case I(A)(1) of the Ministry of External Affairs' (MEA) applicant-category table, the majority pathway for an Indian-resident adult applicant with no prior passport, name change, or pending litigation complications beyond a simple pending-criminal-case declaration. Application is submitted through the Passport Seva Online Portal and finalised at an in-person appointment at a Passport Seva Kendra (PSK), Post Office PSK (POPSK), District Passport Cell (DPC), Speed Post Centre (SPC), or Citizen Service Centre (CSC), where documents are verified and biometrics/photograph captured; a paper Passport Application Form and Supplementary Form (ICR-scanned) remain in use at non-PSK collection centres and share the same columns modelled here. This document models the applicant-detail, family-detail, address, and service-option fields common to every Case I(A)(1) applicant. It does not model: minor applicants (Case I(B), which requires parent/guardian consent per Annexure C/D and is a distinct schema); re-issue of an existing/expired/lost/damaged passport; citizenship by descent, registration, or naturalization (Case I(A)(2)/(3), which require different supporting documents); diplomatic/official passport holders; or a single authoritative fee figure, since the fee schedule (Table 4) changes over time and is not encoded here as a fixed amount, consistent with de/bmi/passport-application and nl/rvig/passport-application. It does not submit the application; the live passportindia.gov.in Passport Seva portal and the Regional Passport Office are always authoritative — see VERIFICATION.md.

authority.name: Ministry of External Affairs, Government of India (Passport Seva / Regional Passport Office), under the Passports Act, 1967

source.documentRef: Passport Application Form and Supplementary Form — Instructions Booklet V3.0 (Ministry of External Affairs)

verification.notes: Source-derived reference schema authored directly from the MEA's official 'Application Form Instruction Booklet V3.0' PDF (fetched directly, HTTP 200), which gives column-by-column guidance for the Passport Application Form and Supplementary Form, the applicant-category table (Table 2), the overall document list (Table 3), and the fee list (Table 4). The PDF renders its text via embedded CID-keyed fonts that a naive content-stream (Tj/TJ paren) regex extraction cannot decode (it recovers only kerning-array whitespace); a scratch npm install of pdfjs-dist (legacy build, Node require) was used instead to run its font glyph-to-Unicode mapping and extract full page text — a new technique for this class of PDF, distinct from the raster-and-read technique used for GOV-634's glyph-obfuscated PDF and the zlib-stream/paren-regex technique used elsewhere, applicable when a PDF has real embedded-font text (not images) but non-trivial CID encoding poppler/pdftoppm is unavailable to rasterize. The instruction booklet itself states field guidance narratively ('put a cross in the appropriate box') without listing exact enum strings for gender/marital status/employment type; those three enumerations were additionally cross-checked against a raw (non-AI-summarised) curl fetch of bankbazaar.com's independent field-by-field summary of the live application form, which lists 'Gender: Male, Female, Transgender', 'Marital Status: Single, Married, Divorced, Widow/Widower, Separated', and the employment-type list verbatim — a secondary corroborating source, not the primary MEA source, flagged here honestly per the same source-fidelity discipline used for prior schemas' caveats. Status remains 'draft' pending a live-portal field-by-field pass. See VERIFICATION.md.

fields.name: previouslyHeldIndianPassport
fields.label: Have you ever held an Indian passport, including one that has since expired?
fields.description: A 'Fresh Passport' application (Column 1.1) is only for an applicant who has never held a passport. An applicant who has previously held one uses a 're-issue of passport' application instead — not modelled in this document.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 1: Service Required — 'Put a cross against Fresh Passport if you have never held a passport'

fields.name: applicantIsMinor
fields.label: Is the applicant under 18 years of age?
fields.description: A minor applicant (Case I(B)) requires parent/legal-guardian consent (Annexure C or D) and a different supporting-document set, and must attend with the consenting parent/guardian — a distinct schema, not modelled here.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Table 2 — Case I(B) 'Minor'; Annexure C/D

fields.name: citizenshipBasis
fields.label: Citizenship of India by
fields.description: birth = born an Indian citizen (Case I(A)(1), modelled in this document). descent = born to Indian parent(s) outside India (Case I(A)(2)). registration_naturalization = citizenship granted by the Ministry of Home Affairs (Case I(A)(3)). The descent and registration/naturalization pathways require different supporting documents (Document No. 4/5/6, or 7) and are not modelled here.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.8: 'Citizenship of India by' — birth, descent, registration, or naturalization; Table 2 Case I(A)(1)/(2)/(3)

fields.name: surname
fields.label: Surname
fields.description: Leave blank if you do not use a surname; write your full name in Given Name instead. Some embassies (e.g. UAE, European countries) insist on a surname for visa issuance.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.1: Applicant's Given Name & Surname

fields.name: givenName
fields.label: Given name
fields.description: Full given name as it should appear on the passport, in CAPITAL LETTERS, with no initials or honorifics/titles.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.1: Applicant's Given Name & Surname

fields.name: hasAlias
fields.label: Are you known by any other name (alias)?
fields.description: For example, if your name is Rajesh Bansal and your alias is Raja, answer yes and give the alias in the Supplementary Form.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.2: Are you known by any other names (aliases)?

fields.name: aliasGivenName
fields.label: Alias given name
fields.description: Given name portion of the alias, per Supplementary Form Column 1.1.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Supplementary Form Column 1.1: Alias Name1, Given Name & Surname

fields.name: hasNameEverChanged
fields.label: Have you ever changed your name?
fields.description: Applies to any change, even marginal (e.g. Harvinder to Harjinder), and to a name/surname changed on marriage.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.3: Have you ever changed your name?

fields.name: previousGivenName
fields.label: Previous given name
fields.description: Given name portion of the name held before the change, per Supplementary Form Column 2.1.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Supplementary Form Column 2.1: Previous Name1, Given Name & Surname

fields.name: dateOfBirth
fields.label: Date of birth
fields.description: DD-MM-YYYY on the source form; represented here as YYYY-MM-DD. Requires an attached proof-of-date-of-birth document.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.4: Date of Birth

fields.name: placeOfBirthCountry
fields.label: Country of birth
fields.description: If born before the partition of India (15 August 1947) at a place now in Pakistan or Bangladesh, write 'Undivided India' as the place of birth and leave District/State blank.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.5: Place of Birth

fields.name: placeOfBirthCity
fields.label: Place of birth (village/town/city)
fields.description: Only required if born in India. Leave blank if born outside India.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.5: Place of Birth — 'if born in India, write the place of birth ... under the respective headings'

fields.name: placeOfBirthDistrict
fields.label: District of birth
fields.description: Only required if born in India.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.5: Place of Birth

fields.name: placeOfBirthState
fields.label: State/Union Territory of birth
fields.description: Only required if born in India.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.5: Place of Birth

fields.name: gender
fields.label: Gender
fields.description: Put a cross on the appropriate box.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.6; corroborated verbatim ('Gender: Male, Female, Transgender') against a raw fetch of bankbazaar.com's application-form field summary

fields.name: maritalStatus
fields.label: Marital status
fields.description: Put a cross on the appropriate box. Married applicants must also give a spouse name.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.7; corroborated verbatim ('Marital Status: Single, Married, Divorced, Widow/Widower, Separated') against a raw fetch of bankbazaar.com's application-form field summary

fields.name: panNumber
fields.label: PAN (if available)
fields.description: Permanent Account Number issued by the Income Tax Department, if the applicant has one.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.9

fields.name: voterIdNumber
fields.label: Voter ID (if available)
fields.description: Elector's Photo Identity Card (EPIC) number issued by the Election Commission of India, if the applicant has one.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.10

fields.name: employmentType
fields.label: Employment type
fields.description: Put a cross on the appropriate box. Selecting a government/PSU/statutory-body category requires an Identity Certificate (Annexure A) from the employer.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.11; corroborated verbatim against a raw fetch of bankbazaar.com's application-form field summary

fields.name: employerOrganizationName
fields.label: Name of organization (if employed in Government/Statutory Body/PSU)
fields.description: Name of the Government office, Statutory Body, or Public Sector Undertaking employing the applicant.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.12

fields.name: isSpouseGovernmentServant
fields.label: Is your spouse a government servant?
fields.description: Affects which supporting documents are required (see Section D of the source booklet). Applies to the applicant's spouse; the equivalent question for a minor applicant's parent is out of scope of this adult-only document.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.13: Is either of your parent (in case of minor)/spouse, a government servant?

fields.name: educationalQualification
fields.label: Educational qualification
fields.description: Highest educational qualification, as selected on the source form. Modelled here as free text because the source booklet directs the applicant to 'put a cross in the appropriate box' without enumerating the box labels; a live-portal pass is needed to confirm the exact closed list (tracked for a future MINOR update).
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.14: Educational Qualification

fields.name: eligibleForNonECR
fields.label: Are you eligible for Non-ECR (Emigration Check Not Required) category?
fields.description: Non-ECR (formerly ECNR) status is available to, among others, matriculation-and-above qualification holders, income-tax payers, professional degree holders, and persons over 50. If not eligible, the passport is issued in the ECR category, printed on the passport. A true answer requires a supporting Non-ECR eligibility document.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.15: Are you eligible for Non-ECR Category?

fields.name: distinguishingMark
fields.label: Visible distinguishing mark
fields.description: E.g. a mole or birth mark on the forehead. Leave blank if none.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.16: Visible distinguishing mark

fields.name: aadhaarNumber
fields.label: Aadhaar number (if available)
fields.description: 12-digit Aadhaar number issued by the Unique Identification Authority of India (UIDAI). Furnishing it can expedite processing and it may also serve as proof of address/identity.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 2.17: Aadhaar Number

fields.name: fatherGivenName
fields.label: Father's given name
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 3: Family Details

fields.name: motherGivenName
fields.label: Mother's given name
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 3: Family Details

fields.name: legalGuardianName
fields.label: Legal guardian's name (if applicable)
fields.description: Only applicable where a legal guardian, rather than a parent, has custody. Requires an attached Court decree/order.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 3: Family Details — 'You need to attach Court decree/order in respect of your legal guardian'

fields.name: spouseName
fields.label: Spouse's name
fields.description: As written in the spouse's own passport, if the spouse holds one.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 3.4 — 'If your spouse has a passport, write his/her name in Column 3.4 as written in the passport'

fields.name: presentAddressLine1
fields.label: Present residential address — House No. and Street Name
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 4.2: House No. and Street Name

fields.name: presentAddressCity
fields.label: Present address — City/Town/Village
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 4: Present Residential Address Details

fields.name: presentAddressState
fields.label: Present address — State/Union Territory
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 4: Present Residential Address Details

fields.name: presentAddressPinCode
fields.label: Present address — PIN code
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 4: Present Residential Address Details

fields.name: permanentAddressSameAsPresent
fields.label: Is your permanent address the same as your present address?
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 4.3: Is permanent address same as present address?

fields.name: permanentAddressLine1
fields.label: Permanent address — House No. and Street Name
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Supplementary Form Column 3: Permanent Residential Address (If it is different from present residential address)

fields.name: emergencyContactName
fields.label: Emergency contact — name
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 5: Emergency Contact Details

fields.name: emergencyContactPhone
fields.label: Emergency contact — phone number
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 5: Emergency Contact Details

fields.name: emergencyContactAddress
fields.label: Emergency contact — address
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 5: Emergency Contact Details

fields.name: hasPendingCriminalCase
fields.label: Is a criminal case pending against you in any court?
fields.description: A pending criminal case does not bar an application, but the applicant must enclose written court permission to travel abroad; a short-validity (typically one year) passport is normally issued instead.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 7: Other Details

fields.name: processingSpeed
fields.label: Type of application
fields.description: normal = standard processing. tatkaal = expedited scheme; no proof of urgency is required and Post-Passport-Issuance police verification applies in all Tatkaal cases; a higher fee applies.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 1.4: Type of Application

fields.name: passportBookletType
fields.label: Passport booklet size
fields.description: 36-page or 60-page booklet; the 60-page booklet carries a higher fee.
fields.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 1.5: Type of Passport Booklet

documents.label: Proof of present residential address
documents.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Table 3, Document No. 1: Proof of Present Address

documents.label: Proof of date of birth
documents.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Table 3, Document No. 2: Proof of Date of Birth

documents.label: Documentary proof of eligibility for Non-ECR category
documents.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Table 3, Document No. 3; Column 2.15

documents.label: Identity Certificate from employer (Annexure 'A')
documents.issuingAuthority: The applicant's Central/State Government department, Statutory Body, or Public Sector Undertaking employer
documents.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Annexure 'A'; Table 3, Document No. 45

documents.label: Court permission to travel abroad
documents.statement: Written permission granted by the Court allowing the applicant to travel abroad notwithstanding the pending criminal case.
documents.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Column 7: Other Details

documents.label: Passport application fee payment
documents.sourceRef: Instructions Booklet V3.0, Section E: Fee List; Column 8 — payment method depends on the submission location (cash only at PSK/POPSK; cash or demand draft at SPC; demand draft only at DPC; cash or card at CSC). Fee amount varies by booklet size, validity, and Tatkaal election (Table 4) and changes over time; not encoded here as a single figure, consistent with de/bmi/passport-application and nl/rvig/passport-application.

steps.title: Eligibility for the fresh-passport, adult, citizen-by-birth pathway
